Title: Maize nicotinate N-methyltransferase interacts with the NLR protein Rp1-D21 and modulates the hypersensitive response

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses Rp1-D21- and CCD21-mediated hypersensitive response (auto-HR)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reported in plants and animals; not directly tested here for defence output
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript slightly up-regulated in maize lines carrying Rp1-D21 versus wild type in B73×H95 and Mo17×H95 backgrounds; cloned from B73 seedlings at the four-leaf stage
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Agrobacterium-mediated transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; HR phenotyping; ion leakage assay; co-immunoprecipitation; yeast two-hybrid; subcellular localization by confocal microscopy; mutational/truncation analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium transient expression in N. benthamiana; greenhouse growth at 24°C, 16 h light/8 h dark for N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (previous analysis referenced in paper; accession not provided)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmNANMT, but not the close homolog ZmCOMT, suppresses Rp1-D21-mediated auto-HR in N. benthamiana. 2. ZmNANMT also suppresses CCD21-mediated auto-HR and physically interacts with CCD21. 3. ZmNANMT interacts with HCT1806 and CCoAOMT2 and together forms complex(es) with CCD21. 4. Mutation of the conserved catalytic residue T278H does not abolish suppression, suggesting NANMT enzymatic activity is not required for HR suppression. 5. Either nuclear- or cytoplasm-enriched localization remains sufficient for suppression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate maize NLR-mediated defence signaling and cell-death intensity; caution because evidence is from heterologous transient assays rather than stable maize genetics
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ZmNANMT is a metabolic enzyme for nicotinate methylation to trigonelline, in this study its core demonstrated role is as a negative regulator of NLR-triggered HR rather than direct alteration of metabolite accumulation. Not a general suppressor of cell death because it does not suppress MLA10(D502V), RPM1(D505V), INF1, or Bax-induced cell death.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: H95-related Rp1-D21 maize lines; exact source allele background not specified
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Immune signaling activation causing hypersensitive response; autoactive allele triggers constitutive HR
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Defence-associated cell death rather than nutrient metabolism directly
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Autoactive Rp1-D21 induces hypersensitive response, which is suppressed by ZmNANMT, HCT1806, and CCoAOMT2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central trigger of the auto-HR phenotype used as readout in this study
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms complex(es) with ZmNANMT, HCT1806, and CCoAOMT2 through its CC domain
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression auto-HR assay; ion leakage; co-IP
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium transient expression in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rp1-D21 is the autoactive maize NLR used to assay HR suppression. 2. Its HR is specifically suppressed by ZmNANMT but not by ZmCOMT. 3. The CC signaling domain is sufficient for HR and interacts with ZmNANMT.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a primary experimental subject, but it is not a nutrient-metabolism gene.
